sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES version

special_mechanics.name: air_pump
special_mechanics.description: Dig Dug stops moving and throws air pump forward to catch and inflate enemies until they explode. Enemy freezes when hit. Repeated button presses inflate further. Pump disconnects if no action taken or player moves; enemy then deflates and stuns.

special_mechanics.name: rock_falling
special_mechanics.description: Rocks fall after tile directly beneath is removed and Dig Dug moves away. Falling rocks destroy enemies on contact but can also crush Dig Dug. Rocks are destroyed upon landing.

special_mechanics.name: enemy_phase_through
special_mechanics.description: Enemies can move through tiles as ghostly eyes, becoming invulnerable, slowed, and unable to attack. They return to solid form in empty spaces.

tier_notes: Tier 2 data: No precise physics parameters documented in Wikipedia source. Dig Dug is primarily a maze/puzzle game with grid-based movement rather than physics-driven platforming. Movement mechanics are not quantified in available documentation.
